rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MARILYN SILVERMAN-BALLONOFF
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Appearnce At Deposition, For Deposition Testimony, And For Production Of Requested Documents; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Against Selene Ballonoff
rulings.Rulings: Thomas Lucas's Motion for Order Compelling Appearance at Deposition, for Deposition Testimony, and for Production of Requested Documents is GRANTED. Selene Ballonoff is ordered to appear for her deposition on or before May 26, 2016 and must bring updated account statements for the disputed funds, as requested in the Notice of Deposition served April 5, 2016. Selene Ballonoff is also ordered to pay monetary sanctions in the amount of $860 to the Law Offices of Daniel Conrad. If Ms. Ballonoff fails to appear for her deposition and/or fails to bring the required documents, the Court will consider imposing evidentiary sanctions pursuant to C.C.P. § 2023.030.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DANIEL P FREDINBURG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To 850 Petition To Determine Title To Real Property And Require Transfer Of Real Property To Limited Liability Company
rulings.Rulings: Paul Fredinburg's Demurrer to the Petition to Determine Title to Real Property and Require Transfer of Real Property to Limited Liability company is overruled. The demurrer is moot in light of the filing of the Amended Petition to Determine Title to Real Property and Require Transfer of Real Property to Limited Liability Company on April 15, 2016.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DANIEL P FREDINBURG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Amended 850 Petition To Determine Title To Real Property And Require Transfer Of Real Property To Limited Liability Company
rulings.Rulings: Paul Fredinburg's Demurrer to the Amended 850 Petition to Determine Title to Real Property and Require Transfer of Real Property to Limited Liability Company is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Though the Court must liberally construe the petition on demurrer and draw inferences favorable to the plaintiff, Petitioners must allege additional facts regarding the implied contract claim underlying their section 850 claim. The implied contract claim (upon which the Decedent's supposed inter vivos transfer of his "beneficial interest" is necessarily based), is only intimated in the FAP at this point, and needs to pleaded with further detail and specificity. To only refer to "transfer of beneficial interest" without alleging the basis of that transfer (i.e., the existence of some form of contract) is incomplete and insufficient, even in light of the deferential standards afforded a plaintiff on demurrer. The prevailing party shall submit a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than 5/17/2016, at 4:00 p.m.
